destinations.evidence: The constructor stores the supplied identity fields, marks the executor active, and records creation time.

equivalence_argument: ExecutorInfo::new preserves the supplied executor metadata and initializes is_active to true with registration_time set at construction.
rationale: The new constructor explicitly initializes executor metadata and sets is_active to true, making the active-by-default state part of the registry model added by this commit. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Constructing an ExecutorInfo with ExecutorInfo::new stores the supplied address, public key, registration block, and registration transaction hash, marks the executor active, and records a registration timestamp at creation time.
suggested_falsification_tests: Instantiate ExecutorInfo::new with known address, public key, block, and tx hash; assert those fields are preserved, is_active is true, and registration_time falls between timestamps captured immediately before and after construction.
summary: New executor records start active in the port registry model.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Construct ExecutorInfo::new with fixed fields and assert address/public_key/block/tx hash are preserved, is_active is true, and registration_time is between two sampled timestamps.
title: New executor records start active
